title: "Find free help" pointers (211 / legal aid / agency lines) integrated without collecting PII
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task integrates "find free help" pointers (211, legal aid, agency lines) into the delivery surface so every guide routes people to free human help - without collecting any PII or tracking who clicks.
objective: Integrate "find free help" pointers (211 / legal aid / agency lines) into the site and guides without collecting PII.
acceptanceCriteria: Every guide surfaces correct free-help pointers (211 / legal aid / agency lines; qualified counsel/accredited rep for immigration) alongside the official application link
No PII is collected and no tracking of help-pointer interactions occurs; privacy review confirms
Free-help pointers are validated against the provenance/staleness fail-safe so outdated pointers are flagged
